---
title: "Chapter 8 - Akshara-Brahma Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">अक्षरब्रह्मयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-07.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-09.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-8-1" data-reference="8.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-1">8.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span> said: What is that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>? What is adhyatma? What is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." aria-label="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." title="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." data-glossary-id="karma">karma</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purushottama</span>? What is called adhibhuta, and what is meant by adhidaiva?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">किं तद् ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम ।<br>
अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-2" data-reference="8.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-2">8.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">How, and who, is the adhiyajna in this body, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusūdana</span>? And how are You to be known at the time of death by the self-controlled?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन ।<br>
प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-3" data-reference="8.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-3">8.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The imperishable is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, the supreme; svabhava is called adhyatma. The offering that brings about the arising of beings is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." aria-label="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." title="karma: Action, deed, or the consequences bound to action." data-glossary-id="karma">karma</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते ।<br>
भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-4" data-reference="8.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-4">8.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Adhibhuta is the perishable nature, and the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purusha</span> is Adhidaivata; and in this body, I alone am the Adhiyajna, O best of embodied beings.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् ।<br>
अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-5" data-reference="8.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-5">8.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And at the time of death, whoever, remembering Me alone, leaves the body and departs, attains My state; there is no doubt about it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् ।<br>
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-6" data-reference="8.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-6">8.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever state one remembers when leaving the body at the end, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kaunteya</span>, one attains that very state, being always absorbed in that state.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् ।<br>
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-7" data-reference="8.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-7">8.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore, remember Me at all times and fight. With mind and intellect offered to Me, you will surely come to Me, without doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च ।<br>
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-8" data-reference="8.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-8">8.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With the mind fixed in practice and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, not wandering elsewhere, one attains the supreme divine <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purusha</span> by constant contemplation, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Pārtha</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना ।<br>
परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-9" data-reference="8.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-9">8.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whoever remembers the omniscient, ancient, ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, the supporter of all, of inconceivable form, radiant like the sun, beyond darkness,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कविं पुराणमनुशासितार-<br>
मणोरणीयंसमनुस्मरेद्यः ।<br>
सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूप-<br>
मादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-10" data-reference="8.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-10">8.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">At the time of departure, with the mind unmoving, and endowed with devotion and indeed with the power of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, properly fixing the life-breath between the eyebrows, one attains that divine Supreme Person.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन<br>
भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव ।<br>
भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्<br>
स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-11" data-reference="8.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-11">8.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I shall now briefly explain to you that imperishable state which the knowers of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Veda</span> call the imperishable, into which the ascetics enter free from attachment, and which those who seek it practice brahmacharya to attain.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति<br>
विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः ।<br>
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति<br>
तत्ते पदं सङ्ग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-12" data-reference="8.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-12">8.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Restraining all the gates, and confining the mind in the heart, placing the life-breath in the head, he remains steadfast in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> concentration.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च ।<br>
मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-13" data-reference="8.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-13">8.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Uttering the one-syllable <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>, and remembering Me, he who departs, leaving the body, attains the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् ।<br>
यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-14" data-reference="8.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-14">8.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> who, with an undivided mind, constantly and always remembers Me, I am easy to attain, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, for one who is ever devoted.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः ।<br>
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-15" data-reference="8.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-15">8.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having attained Me, the great-souled ones no longer come to rebirth, which is a home of sorrow and impermanence; they have attained the supreme perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् ।<br>
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-16" data-reference="8.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-16">8.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, all worlds up to the realm of Brahma are subject to return; but having come to Me, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, there is no rebirth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन ।<br>
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-17" data-reference="8.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-17">8.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who know the day of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> declare it to last for a thousand yugas, and the night, for the end of a thousand yugas; those are the people who know day and night.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद् ब्रह्मणो विदुः ।<br>
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-18" data-reference="8.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-18">8.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">At the coming of day, all manifestations arise from the unmanifest; and at the coming of night, they dissolve back into that same unmanifest.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अव्यक्ताद् व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे ।<br>
रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-19" data-reference="8.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-19">8.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This same multitude of beings, again and again coming into being, is dissolved; at the coming of night it is helpless, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Pārtha</span>, and at the coming of day it comes forth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते ।<br>
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-20" data-reference="8.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-20">8.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But beyond that is another, unmanifest, eternal reality, which is higher than the unmanifest.<br>
It does not perish when all beings perish.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः ।<br>
यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-21" data-reference="8.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-21">8.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The unmanifest is called the imperishable; they speak of it as the supreme goal. Attaining it, they do not return—that is My supreme abode.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् ।<br>
यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-22" data-reference="8.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-22">8.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">That Supreme Person, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is attainable by single-minded devotion. All beings dwell within Him, and by Him all this is pervaded.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया ।<br>
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-23" data-reference="8.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-23">8.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I shall tell you, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bhāratas</span>, of the time when the yogins who depart attain non-return, and also the time when they return.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः ।<br>
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-24" data-reference="8.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-24">8.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fire, light, day, the bright fortnight, the six months of the northward course—by these, those who depart and know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> go to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् ।<br>
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-25" data-reference="8.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-25">8.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Smoke, night, and likewise the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southern course—there the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogī</span>, attaining the lunar light, returns.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् ।<br>
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-26" data-reference="8.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-26">8.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">These two, the bright and the dark, are indeed held to be the world’s eternal paths; by one one goes on without returning, by the other one returns again.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते ।<br>
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-27" data-reference="8.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-27">8.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Pārtha</span>, no yogin who knows these two paths is deluded. Therefore, be united with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> at all times, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन ।<br>
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-8-28" data-reference="8.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-8-28">8.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever meritorious fruit is declared in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, in sacrifices, in austerities, and in gifts—having known all this, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> surpasses it all and attains the supreme, primordial abode.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव<br>
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् ।<br>
अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा<br>
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
